skill.name: azure-role-selector
skill.description: Helps choose least-privilege Azure role assignments for an identity based on requested permissions, then explains how to apply them.
skill.summary: Select least-privilege Azure roles and generate assignment guidance.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported five high-severity weak cryptography matches in SKILL.md, but the referenced lines describe Azure RBAC role selection and role assignment guidance. I found no cryptographic implementation, prompt injection attempt, credential access, file access, or data exfiltration behavior in the reviewed skill content.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 6 appear to match the word role or related Azure RBAC text, not a weak cryptographic algorithm. The skill contains guidance to use Azure MCP documentation and generation tools for least-privilege role selection, with no hashing, cipher, encryption, or signature logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only reviewed file has six lines and contains Azure role assignment instructions, not cryptographic code. No evidence of weak algorithms or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Azure RBAC choices can be difficult when permissions must be precise. This skill uses Azure MCP references to identify the smallest suitable role and produce assignment guidance.
actual_capabilities: Finds a minimal Azure built-in role that matches requested permissions.
Suggests a custom role definition when no built-in role matches.
Generates Azure CLI role assignment commands through Azure MCP tooling.
Provides Bicep role assignment snippets using Azure schema guidance.
Applies Azure best-practice guidance when explaining role assignment choices.
limitations: Depends on the configured Azure MCP tools for documentation and generation.
Does not execute role assignments or change Azure resources directly.
Cannot confirm tenant-specific policies unless the user provides that context.
Generated role guidance should be reviewed before production deployment.

use_cases.title: Grant Storage Read Access
use_cases.description: Identify the smallest Azure role for an app identity that only needs to read blobs.

use_cases.title: Design Custom RBAC
use_cases.description: Create a custom role when built-in Azure roles include more permissions than requested.

use_cases.title: Document Access Changes
use_cases.description: Generate CLI and Bicep guidance for a planned role assignment change request.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Basic Role
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one identity and one resource scope.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the least-privilege Azure role for an identity that needs read-only access to blobs in one storage account.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Built-In Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that needs a short role comparison.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare built-in Azure roles for managing virtual machine start and stop operations without allowing delete permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Assignment Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the minimal Azure role for a managed identity, then provide Azure CLI assignment steps and a Bicep role assignment snippet.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Role
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request when built-in roles may be too broad.
prompt_templates.prompt: Determine whether a built-in Azure role matches these exact permissions. If none match, draft a custom role and explain the assignment approach.

output_examples.input: An application needs to read blobs from one storage account and must not write or delete data.
output_examples.output: Recommends Storage Blob Data Reader at the storage account scope, explains why broader contributor roles are unnecessary, and provides assignment guidance.

output_examples.input: A support team needs to restart virtual machines but must not edit disks, networking, or access policies.
output_examples.output: Compares relevant built-in roles, flags overbroad options, and suggests a custom role if the exact permission set is not available.

output_examples.input: Create deployment guidance for assigning a role to a managed identity using infrastructure as code.
output_examples.output: Provides a role recommendation, Azure CLI assignment steps, and a Bicep-oriented description suitable for review.

best_practices: Provide the target identity, resource scope, and exact required actions.
Ask for a comparison when several built-in roles appear close.
Review generated CLI and Bicep guidance before applying it in Azure.
anti_patterns: Requesting broad owner or contributor access without listing required permissions.
Using subscription-wide scope when resource-level scope is sufficient.
Applying generated custom roles without security or platform review.

faq.question: Does this skill assign Azure roles directly?
faq.answer: No. It generates guidance and command examples, but it does not execute Azure changes.

faq.question: Can it recommend custom Azure roles?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest a custom role when no built-in role matches the requested permissions.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the identity type, resource scope, required actions, and permissions that must be excluded.

faq.question: Does it support Bicep output?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to provide Bicep role assignment guidance using Azure MCP schema information.

faq.question: Is it limited to built-in Azure roles?
faq.answer: No. It starts with built-in roles and can move to custom role guidance when needed.

faq.question: Should I use the output in production without review?
faq.answer: No. Review generated role assignments against your Azure policies and change management process.
